name: Pan-Fried Noodles
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch the fresh noodles in boiling water for about 2 minutes to wash away starch and loosen them up, then drain well.
2. Scatter the noodles in a large skillet. Untangle them with chopsticks and your hands, spreading them evenly in layers (about 1 inch deep) in two separate skillets.
3. Pour 1 tablespoon peanut oil and 2/3 cup chicken broth into each skillet. Cook over low flame until the broth boils away and the bottom browns slightly.
4. Occasionally add a little oil and broth to keep noodles moist and allow the crust to develop slowly for about 10-12 minutes.
5. Flip using chopsticks: shake the pan first, then carefully flip the skillet with a lid, letting it drop onto the plate. Return to the pan if needed to brown on the other side.
6. For the pork and vegetable sauce, heat oil in a wok, stir-fry bok choy until coated, add pork pieces, cook through, then mix in broth, soy sauce, rice wine/sherry, and oyster sauce. Thicken with cornstarch dissolved in cold water before serving over the pan-fried noodles.
